subject: Safari cannot open most websites
description: User reports Safari spins or shows 'cannot connect' on multiple sites; other browsers not tried.

ts_steps.title: Clarify failure scope
ts_steps.action: Ask whether every HTTPS site fails or only intranet, captive portal, or specific domains. Record exact error text (cannot connect, certificate, timeout).

ts_steps.title: Baseline reachability
ts_steps.action: In Safari try https://www.apple.com and one other major HTTPS site. Optionally Terminal: ping -c 4 1.1.1.1 and ping -c 4 a known DNS name to separate L3 vs DNS vs TLS.

ts_steps.title: Captive portal and VPN
ts_steps.action: Open a deliberate http:// URL to trigger guest Wi-Fi portal if present. Disconnect VPN and retest. Note menu bar Wi-Fi for limited connectivity warnings.

ts_steps.title: Date and time
ts_steps.action: System Settings → General → Date & Time → enable Set time and date automatically; confirm time zone. Wrong clock breaks TLS handshakes.

ts_steps.title: Wi-Fi and DNS
ts_steps.action: System Settings → Network → Wi-Fi → Details → TCP/IP → Renew DHCP Lease. Under DNS add a public resolver temporarily for test (e.g. 1.1.1.1) if policy allows, Apply, retest Safari, then revert if required.

ts_steps.title: Toggle network interface
ts_steps.action: Toggle Wi-Fi off 10 seconds on, or unplug/replug Ethernet. Disable other active interfaces briefly if multiple routes confuse traffic.

ts_steps.title: Safari website data
ts_steps.action: Safari → Settings → Privacy → Manage Website Data — remove failing domains or Remove All for broad test. Quit Safari fully, reopen.

ts_steps.title: Private Window and cache
ts_steps.action: File → New Private Window — load same URLs. Safari → Clear History for last hour if policy allows.

ts_steps.title: Extensions off
ts_steps.action: Safari → Settings → Extensions — disable all, quit Safari, relaunch, retest.

ts_steps.title: New user or escalation
ts_steps.action: Create a test user account and try Safari once to rule out profile plist issues. If all browsers fail, escalate proxy/PAC/TLS inspection with macOS version and Safari build.

internal_information: Time, DNS, captive portal, VPN, extensions, corrupt site data. Apple Silicon only. Corporate TLS inspection may need cert trust — not Safari-only.
